commonOptions.title: Common Options:
commonOptions.envelope: Only available with --format json, outputs full envelope wrapper

session.description: List sessions (all or filtered by agent)
session.summary: List sessions (all or filtered by agent)

send.summary: Send message to agent session and wait for reply

index.summary: Scan artifact directory and rebuild index.jsonl, used to include historical files after upgrade or fix index inconsistency

list.summary: List artifacts with status/type/batch/cursor pagination support

export.summary: Export artifacts as date->pipeline->node three-level JSON

cleanup.summary: Clean up old artifact files, default dry-run shows candidates without deleting, pass --confirm to execute real deletion and rebuild index
cleanup.optConfirm: Must be explicitly passed to execute real deletion, otherwise dry-run
cleanup.dryRunMessage: Dry-run mode, candidate files will not be deleted. Pass --confirm to execute real deletion.

start.description: Start a pipeline run (completion not guaranteed)
start.summary: Start a pipeline run (completion not guaranteed)
start.optWatch: Continue waiting for run completion after start
start.optInterval: Watch polling interval in ms, default 1200
start.noteOnlyStart: start only initiates the run request, does not guarantee completion when the command returns.
start.noteWatch: --watch enters the wait flow after a successful start.

status.argPipelineId: Compatibility entry, query current run by pipeline ID
status.optBatchRunId: Query by specific batch run ID
status.noteNeedSelector: At least one of <pipelineId>, --run-id, --batch-run-id must be provided.
status.noteDefaultQuery: When no selector is provided, defaults to querying current active run by pipelineId.

watch.description: Watch pipeline until completion or timeout
watch.summary: Watch pipeline until completion or timeout
watch.argPipelineId: Compatibility entry, watch current run of this pipeline
watch.optBatchRunId: Watch by specific batch run ID
watch.optInterval: Watch polling interval in ms, default 1200
watch.noteIsWatch: watch is a monitoring command, it does not start new runs.
watch.noteNeedSelector: At least one of <pipelineId>, --run-id, --batch-run-id must be provided.
watch.notePollingFallback: Current watch semantics prioritize event streams with polling as fallback.

stop.argPipelineId: Compatibility entry, stop current run of this pipeline
stop.optBatchRunId: Stop by specific batch run ID
stop.noteNeedSelector: At least one of <pipelineId>, --run-id, --batch-run-id must be provided.
stop.noteBatchOnly: Currently stop is mainly used for stopping batch runs.
stop.noteNonBatch: Non-batch runs will return a business error instead of silent success.

retryNode.description: Retry a node or node item
retryNode.summary: Retry a node or node item
retryNode.optItem: Specify the item key to retry

diagnose.description: Diagnose node dependency status, output blocking reason
diagnose.summary: Diagnose node dependency status, output blocking reason
diagnose.optItem: Optional, specify item key for precise diagnosis

result.summary: Show artifact content of each node after pipeline run

ensure.description: Ensure local control-plane backend is started and healthy
ensure.summary: Ensure local control-plane daemon is started and healthy
ensure.noteDaemonFirst: Daemon-first semantics: prefer reusing an existing healthy local instance.
ensure.noteNewDaemon: Only starts a new daemon when no reusable healthy instance is available.

status.summary: View local control-plane daemon health, ownership and pid info
status.note: Status output is used to confirm whether local daemon is reusable and current owner metadata is consistent.
